title: 10 best herbal creams for palpitation

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis creams helps with palpitation because they contain valerenic acid, a key compound found in the valerian root. This natural ingredient has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help calm an overactive heart. By reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, valerian root creams can help alleviate palpitations caused by stress and emotional imbalances. Additionally, valerian root has been traditionally used to regulate heartbeat and promote a sense of calm, making it a popular natural remedy for palpitations.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: This valerenic acid helps to reduce anxiety and stress, thereby preventing palpitations caused by emotional factors.
Isovaleryl valerenic acid: This compound has a sedative effect and helps to regulate heart rate, reducing palpitations.
Valtrate: This valtrate has a calming effect on the nervous system, which in turn helps to alleviate palpitations by reducing stress and anxiety.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Valeriana officinalis are used to make creams for palpitations due to their high content of valerenic acids, which have a sedative and calming effect.
Barks: The barks of Valeriana officinalis are sometimes used to make creams for palpitations due to their ability to relax the nervous system and reduce anxiety.
Flowers: The flowers of Valeriana officinalis are used to make creams for palpitations due to their sedative properties and ability to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 valeriana officinalis roots in the morning after the dew has dried off completely.
Dry the roots in a single layer at 30 degrees celsius for 24-48 hours to remove moisture.
Grind 50g of dried root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Steep 100g of the powder in 500ml of cold-pressed carrier oil at 40 degrees celsius for 4-6 weeks.
Strain and decant the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata creams helps with palpitation because they contain extracts from the Passiflora incarnata plant, also known as passionflower. The herb has been traditionally used to treat anxiety and insomnia, which are often underlying causes of palpitations. The flavonoids and alkaloids present in Passiflora incarnata work to reduce stress and promote relaxation, helping to slow down heart rate and alleviate palpitations. Additionally, the cream's soothing properties can calm the nervous system, further contributing to its effectiveness in managing palpitations.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help to reduce palpitations by exerting a sedative effect on the nervous system, leading to a decrease in heart rate.
Harmane: Contributes to the calming effect on the nervous system, which may help to alleviate anxiety and palpitations.
Isoflavones: May help to lower blood pressure and reduce palpitations by acting as a vasodilator and reducing the heart's workload.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their rich content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that can help reduce anxiety and alleviate palpitations.
Barks: The barks are used for their sedative and anxiolytic properties, which can help regulate heart rate and reduce palpitations.
Roots: The roots are used due to their high content of flavonoids and other compounds that can help alleviate anxiety and reduce the risk of palpitations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh or dried passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves for later use in the cream.
Mix 20g of the harvested flowers and leaves with 50g of vegetable oil in a small saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 20 minutes stirring occasionally to infuse the oil.
Strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl.
Mix 20g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter with 30g of the infused oil in a double boiler.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa creams helps with palpitations because they contain extracts from oat plants that have a calming effect on the nervous system. The herb has been used traditionally to soothe anxiety and promote relaxation, which can contribute to reducing palpitations. Avena sativa cream works by regulating the heart rate and blood pressure, helping to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress that can trigger palpitations. This natural remedy provides a gentle yet effective way to manage palpitations and promote overall heart health.
remedy_constituents: Avenanthramides: These polyphenolic compounds have antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, contributing to a decrease in palpitation symptoms.

Ferulic acid: As a phenolic acid, ferulic acid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate palpitation caused by inflammation and oxidative stress in the cardiovascular system.

Gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA): Acting as a neurotransmitter, GABA can help regulate heart rate and blood pressure, potentially reducing palpitation by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety-related cardiovascular symptoms.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used for their saponin content, which helps to calm the nervous system and reduce palpitations.
Leaves: Utilized for their flavonoid and phenolic compounds, which have a soothing effect on the heart and nervous system.
Roots: Employed for their tropane alkaloids, which help to regulate heart rate and alleviate palpitations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried avena sativa roots and leaves and clean them thoroughly.
Mix 50 grams of the cleaned avena sativa with 500 milliliters of carrier oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60-80 degrees celsius for 2-3 hours to achieve a solvent extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and collect the infused oil for further processing.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of vitamin e oil with the infused oil to create a cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with palpitation because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties and ability to regulate the cardiovascular system. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to have a positive effect on heart rate and blood pressure. The calming effects of ginger may also help to reduce anxiety and stress, common triggers for palpitations. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, ginger creams may provide relief from palpitations and promote overall cardiovascular well-being.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols have anti-inflammatory properties that help in reducing stress and anxiety, which can contribute to palpitations.
Shogaols: Shogaols have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help in reducing palpitations by stabilizing heart rate and blood pressure.
6-Gingerol: 6-Gingerol has vasodilatory properties, which can help in improving blood circulation and reducing the workload on the heart, thus alleviating palpitations.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Used for making creams to alleviate palpitations due to its bioactive compounds that exhibit anti-anxiety and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Utilized in creams to treat palpitations because they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids with vasodilatory and anti-arrhythmic effects.
Buds: Employed in creams to address palpitations because of their high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils with potential anti-arrhythmic and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Grate 50g of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes using a microplane grater for 30 seconds.
Combine the grated zingiber officinale with 20g of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat.
Add 50g of sweet almond oil and 10g of coconut oil to the mixture stirring constantly.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool and thicken for 20 minutes.
Strain the cooled mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar discarding solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with palpitation because of its unique ability to improve blood flow and circulation. The herb's active compounds, flavonoids and terpenoids, help to dilate blood vessels and increase oxygen supply to the heart. This enhanced circulation can help to reduce the frequency and severity of palpitations by improving overall cardiovascular health. Additionally, ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties may also help to protect the heart from oxidative stress and inflammation, further contributing to its potential benefits in managing palpitations.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: Helps with palpitations by acting as a calcium channel blocker, which can reduce heart rate and blood pressure.

Quercetin: Helps with palpitations by its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, leading to a decrease in heart rate and blood pressure.

Flavonoids (specifically, kaempferol and isorhamnetin): Helps with palpitations by improving blood flow and reducing platelet aggregation, which can contribute to a decrease in heart rate and blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most widely used part of Ginkgo biloba due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to help alleviate palpitations.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are used to extract bilobalide and ginkgolides, which are thought to have a calming effect on the heart, thereby reducing palpitations.
Barks: The bark of Ginkgo biloba is also used, as it contains flavonoids and terpenoids that are believed to have a sedative effect, which may help alleviate palpitations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in late summer or early fall.
Dry the leaves in a warm dark place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Grind 50g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powder with 100g of coconut oil and 20g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture constantly for 10-15 minutes or until it reaches a smooth consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Centella asiatica creams helps with palpitation because they contain compounds like asiatic acid and madecassic acid, which have a calming effect on the nervous system. These active ingredients help to regulate heart rate and reduce stress-induced palpitations. Additionally, Centella asiatica has antioxidant properties that protect the heart from oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting overall cardiovascular health. By reducing stress and inflammation, Centella asiatica creams help to alleviate palpitations, promoting a sense of calm and well-being. Regular use can lead to improved heart health and reduced palpitation symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Asiatic acid: Helps to reduce palpitations by exerting anti-anxiety and anti-stress effects, promoting a sense of calmness and relaxation.
Madecassoside: Contributes to the reduction of palpitations by acting as a vasodilator, improving blood flow and circulation, and thereby reducing blood pressure.
Madecassic acid: Helps to alleviate palpitations by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the cardiovascular system.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Centella asiatica are commonly used in creams for palpitations due to their high content of triterpenoid saponins, which help to regulate heart rate and reduce anxiety.
Seeds: The seeds are used in creams for palpitations due to their ability to help regulate heart rate and blood pressure, attributed to their high content of triterpenoid saponins.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Centella asiatica are used in creams for palpitations due to their high antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce stress and anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of centella asiatica leaves and flowers from a clean and organic environment for freshness.
Extract the herbs using 500ml of organic carrier oil at a ratio of 1:5 for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids after 2 days of sedimentation.
Mix the extracted oil with 10% beeswax and 10% shea butter to create a stable emulsion.
Whip the mixture in a double boiler at 40°c for 10 minutes to achieve the desired consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis creams helps with palpitation because of its calming and soothing properties. The herb, also known as lemon balm, has been traditionally used to ease anxiety and stress-related symptoms, which are often contributing factors to palpitations. The creams can help regulate heart rate and promote relaxation, reducing the likelihood of palpitations. Furthermore, Melissa officinalis contains flavonoids and terpenes that have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate palpitations and promote a sense of calm.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic acid helps alleviate palpitations by acting as a calcium channel blocker, reducing heart rate and blood pressure.
Luteolin: This flavonoid helps with palpitations by blocking adrenergic receptors, reducing the release of stress hormones such as adrenaline, and promoting relaxation.
Volatile oils (mainly linalool and linalyl acetate): These terpenes help with palpitations by promoting relaxation, reducing anxiety, and acting as a mild sedative to calm the nervous system.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of rosmarinic acid, which helps to regulate heart rate and blood pressure.
Flowers: They are used for their calming and sedative properties, which can help to reduce palpitations caused by anxiety or stress.
Roots: They are used for their ability to lower blood pressure and heart rate, providing a natural remedy for palpitations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves at 30°c for 24 hours using a food dehydrator or a low-temperature oven.
Steep 30g of the dried melissa officinalis leaves in 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids.
Mix 250ml of the strained oil with 50g of beeswax and 50ml of distilled water to create the final cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with palpitation because they contain the calming properties of lavender oil, which can help regulate heart rate and reduce stress. The oil's soothing effects can calm the nervous system, leading to a decrease in palpitation episodes. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of lavender oil may also help alleviate symptoms associated with palpitations, such as anxiety and restlessness. Regular use of lavender creams can promote relaxation and help manage palpitations effectively.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Acts as a natural anxiolytic and sedative, helping to reduce stress and anxiety that can contribute to palpitations.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to regulate heart rate and alleviate palpitations.
Caryophyllene: Has anti-inflammatory properties that may help to reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system, potentially leading to a decrease in palpitations.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are commonly used in creams to treat palpitations due to their calming and soothing properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used to create creams that help alleviate palpitations due to their relaxing and anti-anxiety effects.
Seeds: Although less common, Lavandula angustifolia seeds are sometimes used to create creams for palpitations, possibly due to their potential sedative properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers at the peak of their fragrance and store them properly.
Combine 20-30 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 100ml of a carrier oil in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture for 2-4 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional agitation and shaking.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids and squeezing for oil.
Mix the infused oil with 20-30 grams of beeswax and 10-20 grams of cocoa butter in a double boiler.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with palpitation because they contain licorice root extract, which has been traditionally used to calm the nervous system and regulate heart function. The anti-inflammatory properties in Glycyrrhiza glabra may help reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers for palpitations. Additionally, the cream's soothing effects can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation. This can lead to a reduction in palpitation frequency and severity. Regular use may also help improve overall heart health.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It helps with palpitation by inhibiting the activity of the enzyme 11-beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase, which is involved in the regulation of blood pressure and heart rate, thereby reducing the risk of palpitations.

Isoflavones: They help with palpitation by exerting a vasodilatory effect, which reduces blood pressure and heart rate, and also by modulating the autonomic nervous system, leading to a decrease in palpitations.

Flavonoids: They help with palpitation by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, thereby reducing the risk of palpitations.
remedy_parts: Roots: Glycyrrhiza glabra roots are the most commonly used part in making creams for palpitations due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and cardiac-stimulating properties.
Leaves: Glycyrrhiza glabra leaves are sometimes used in combination with roots to make creams for palpitations due to their potential in improving heart health and reducing stress.
Barks: Glycyrrhiza glabra barks are occasionally used in making creams for palpitations due to their reported ability to help regulate heartbeats and promote overall cardiovascular well-being.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots to use in the herbal cream recipe.
Combine 500 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots with 1 liter of distilled water in a saucepan.
Boil the mixture for 20 minutes to infuse the roots and extract their active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Mix 1 gram of beeswax with 10 grams of coconut oil and 10 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.

remedies.remedy_desc: Centaurium erythraea creams helps with palpitation because they contain the herb's bioactive compounds, which have a calming effect on the nervous system. The herb's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties also contribute to its ability to soothe and regulate heart function. By reducing stress and anxiety, Centaurium erythraea creams can help alleviate palpitations, promoting a sense of calm and well-being. Its natural compounds work to regulate heart rhythm, providing relief from irregular heartbeat and palpitations.
remedy_constituents: Phenylethanoid glycosides: They help with palpitation by exerting a cardiotonic effect, improving heart function and reducing irregular heartbeats.
Iridoid glycosides: They help with palpitation by acting as calcium channel blockers, which can help regulate heart rhythm and reduce palpitations.
Tannins: They help with palpitation by exerting a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and stress that may contribute to palpitations.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Centaurium erythraea are used to make creams for palpitation due to their sedative and anti-anxiety properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Centaurium erythraea are used to make creams for palpitation due to their calming and anti-inflammatory effects.
Roots: The roots of Centaurium erythraea are used to make creams for palpitation due to their cardiac and sedative properties, which help to regulate heartbeat and reduce anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Extract 20g of dried centaurium erythraea root in 100ml of glycerin within 2 hours.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 2 weeks, shaking daily for 10 minutes.
Strain the solution through cheesecloth and discard the solids within 1 hour.
Mix 10g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Combine the herbal extract with the melted wax mixture and stir for 5 minutes.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for palpitation are topical remedies that help alleviate the symptoms of irregular heartbeats and palpitations by promoting relaxation and reducing stress. These creams are often formulated with a blend of herbal extracts, including Valeriana officinalis, Passiflora incarnata, Avena sativa, Zingiber officinale, Ginkgo biloba, Centella asiatica, Melissa officinalis, Lavandula angustifolia, Glycyrrhiza glabra, and Centaurium erythraea. If left untreated, palpitations can lead to anxiety, insomnia, and decreased quality of life, causing individuals to miss out on daily activities, relationships, and experiences due to constant worry and fear of another episode.
